items.prompt: A partition call begins at index start in a string of length n. Which exclusive end boundaries produce non-empty next segments?

options.text: start + 1 through n, inclusive.
start through n - 1.
0 through start.
Only n.

feedback.reason: A non-empty segment [start, end) needs end > start and may extend to the input end.
feedback.details: Each legal boundary is a branch choice before segment validation.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.start_to_n_minus_one: end = start creates an empty segment and end = n must be available.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.zero_to_start: These boundaries overlap or precede the unresolved suffix.

items.prompt: When is a full segmentation complete?

options.text: When the next unresolved start index equals n.
When at least one segment has been chosen.
When a chosen end is less than n.
When the latest segment has length one.

feedback.reason: Completion means the chosen segments cover the input exactly with no gap or remainder.
feedback.details: The exclusive boundary convention makes start == n the natural terminal state.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.path_nonempty: A non-empty path may leave input uncovered.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.segment_length_one: Segment length does not define total coverage.

items.prompt: Why are empty segments usually excluded from ordinary partition enumeration?

options.text: Choosing end = start makes no input progress and can cause infinite recursion or duplicate decompositions.
An empty segment can never be valid in any contract.
Exclusive indices cannot represent empty ranges.
Empty segments prevent sorting.

feedback.reason: Every recursive choice should advance the unresolved boundary unless the contract explicitly models empty components.
feedback.details: Without progress, the state can repeat indefinitely.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.empty_never_valid: Some specialized contracts may allow it; the issue is the ordinary non-empty partition model.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.indices_forbidden: A half-open range [start, start) represents an empty segment.

items.prompt: A palindrome-partition solver considers substring [start, end). When should it recurse from end?

options.text: Only if the chosen substring satisfies the palindrome predicate.
For every possible boundary, even when the segment is invalid.
Only for one-character segments.
Only when end equals n.

feedback.reason: Segment validity is part of candidate legality.
feedback.details: Once the boundary fixes the segment, later choices affect only the suffix and cannot repair that segment.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.always: An invalid fixed segment cannot become valid later.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.only_at_n: Intermediate segments are necessary for multi-part partitions.

items.prompt: After recursively exploring a chosen segment, what must happen before trying another end boundary?

options.text: Remove the segment from the current partition path.
Delete the segment from the original string.
Clear all completed partitions.
Reset start to zero.

feedback.reason: The current path is branch-local state and must return to the parent partition prefix.
feedback.details: The next boundary represents a sibling choice from the same start.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.delete_input: The input remains shared and immutable.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.reset_start_zero: The parent start remains unchanged while its loop tries another end.

items.prompt: Which relation between consecutive segments prevents gaps and overlaps?

options.text: The next segment starts exactly at the previous segment's exclusive end.
Every segment starts at zero.
The next segment starts one position after the previous exclusive end.
The next segment may begin at any index.

feedback.reason: Exact boundary handoff makes the segments contiguous and collectively covering.
feedback.details: Half-open intervals [start, end) compose cleanly when the child start equals end.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.arbitrary_start: Arbitrary starts can create gaps and overlaps.

items.prompt: A solver records a partition as soon as the latest segment is valid, even though unprocessed input remains. What defect results?

options.text: It records incomplete partitions that do not cover the full input.
It automatically removes duplicate partitions.
It changes auxiliary space to O(1).
It sorts all segments.

feedback.reason: Local segment validity is not the full result contract.
feedback.details: A partition becomes recordable only when the recursive start reaches the input length.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.constant_space: Recording timing does not remove path state.

items.prompt: For the string "abc", how many possible cut locations exist between characters?

feedback.reason: A length-n sequence has n - 1 internal boundaries where a cut may be present or absent.
feedback.details: These binary boundary decisions explain the 2^(n - 1) unconstrained partition count.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.three: There are three characters but only two internal gaps.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.one: Both gaps between a-b and b-c are possible cut locations.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.four: Boundaries at the outer ends do not represent optional internal cuts.

items.prompt: When is choosing the entire remaining suffix as one segment legal?

options.text: When [start, n) satisfies the segment predicate.
Always, regardless of segment constraints.
Never; partitions must contain at least two segments.
Only when the suffix is empty.

feedback.reason: The boundary end = n is a normal candidate representing one final segment.
feedback.details: If valid, its child call reaches start = n and records the complete partition.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.always: The segment must satisfy the same validity contract as shorter choices.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.never: A one-segment partition can be valid.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.only_empty: The empty suffix is completion, not an ordinary non-empty segment.

items.prompt: Which conditions make [start, end) a legal next partition segment? Select all that apply.

options.text: end is greater than start.
end is at most the input length.
The segment satisfies the required predicate.
start is reset to zero for every segment.

feedback.reason: A legal segment advances the boundary, stays inside the input, and satisfies the segment contract.
feedback.details: The child starts at end rather than restarting.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.start_reset_zero: That would overlap already covered input.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.end_at_most_n: The range remains inside the input.

items.prompt: Which tests are important for partition-boundary correctness? Select all that apply.

options.text: Empty input under an explicitly defined empty-partition contract.
A one-element input.
An input where both the whole sequence and a multi-segment split are valid.
Only one long random input.

feedback.reason: Boundary review needs identity, minimum non-empty, and competing-cut cases.
feedback.details: A long case alone does not reveal the exact transition between legal and illegal boundaries.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.only_long_random: It may not isolate off-by-one boundary defects.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.single_element: It should have one possible non-empty segment.
omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.whole_and_split: It verifies end = n and shorter boundaries.

items.prompt: Which conditions can soundly prune a partition branch? Select all that apply.

options.text: The newly chosen segment violates a mandatory predicate.
The path already exceeds a hard maximum segment count.
The remaining length cannot satisfy a hard minimum size for the required remaining segments.
The segment looks unusual but satisfies every stated constraint.

feedback.reason: Partition pruning follows from fixed-segment validity and explicit capacity constraints.
feedback.details: Subjective preference may affect ordering but cannot remove a legal branch.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.invalid_fixed_segment: Later suffix choices cannot change it.
omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.too_many_segments: Adding more segments cannot repair the excess.

elements.text: If start equals n, record a snapshot of the current partition.
Enumerate exclusive end boundaries from start + 1 through n.
Check whether [start, end) is a legal segment.
Append the segment, recurse from end, then pop it.

feedback.reason: Completion is resolved before generating segments, and only valid segments become child branches.
feedback.details: The path is restored for each sibling end boundary.

items.prompt: Arrange the operations for one chosen partition boundary.

elements.text: Append the valid segment to the path.
Recurse from end, then remove the segment.

feedback.reason: The boundary defines a candidate segment whose legality is checked before mutation.
feedback.details: Undo returns to the same partition prefix for the next boundary.

items.prompt: Arrange the completion flow when the final chosen segment reaches n.

elements.text: Record a snapshot at the completion base case.
Return and remove the final segment from the live path.

feedback.reason: The final segment becomes part of the completed path before the base case records it.
feedback.details: Popping afterward preserves the stored snapshot while restoring the parent.

items.prompt: Arrange the reasoning for a minimum-segment-length capacity prune.

elements.text: Compute the minimum total length required by the remaining segment slots.
If remaining length is smaller than required length, prune.
Otherwise continue with legal boundary choices.

feedback.reason: The branch is impossible when remaining material cannot satisfy mandatory segment sizes.
feedback.details: The bound must use the exact number of remaining slots and the stated minimum length.

items.prompt: A length-n sequence may be cut or not cut at each internal boundary, and every resulting segment is considered valid. Select the number of complete partitions and maximum recursion depth.
constraints: There are n - 1 internal cut positions.
Each recursive segment consumes at least one element.

feedback.reason: Each internal boundary independently separates or joins adjacent elements.
feedback.details: The deepest partition uses n single-element segments.

items.prompt: A collect-all partition solver returns s partitions, and each partition contains at most n segment references or copied segments. Select a lower bound on materialized output work and output space.
constraints: Every returned partition is stored independently.

feedback.reason: Returning s partitions of size up to n is inherently output-sensitive.
feedback.details: Pruning invalid segments can reduce search overhead but cannot avoid materializing required valid outputs.
